description: Multi-phase plans currently in flight. Each phase references roadmap item titles; the activePhase section breaks the current phase into concrete tasks with status. Survives conversation compactions — a fresh session can pick up by reading this file.

phases.title: Plugin runtime — manifest, SDK, federation hooks
roadmapItems: Plugin runtime — manifest format, loader, SDK, federation hooks
phases.notes: Shipped. PluginManifest / definePlugin / server + client loaders / auto-mounted federation routes / hello-world reference plugin.

phases.title: Polls & Quizzes plugin — first SDK consumer
roadmapItems: Polls & Quizzes — first plugin (validates simple SDK case)
phases.notes: Shipped end-to-end: schema migrations, REST routes, federation handler, real-time sync via babelr:ws custom event, translation wired manually (to be automated in Phase 3), list-detail view via the new ListDetailView primitive, sidebar-slot button opens the view.

phases.title: SDK gap-closure based on polls implementation
roadmapItems: Translation as default for plugin content — zero-config primitive
phases.notes: All four in-scope tasks shipped: component ergonomics (ComponentType + createElement), translation-by-default (<T>), WS helper (onWsMessage), and documented the server-safe/client-only file-split convention. Plugin authors now have one-liners for the friction points polls exposed. Phase 4 (project management) starts on a smoother SDK. Deferred items persisted to roadmap: 'Plugin SDK — stable re-exports of @babelr/client utilities' (P2, ships with Phase 5), 'Plugin DB abstraction — helper over drizzle for plugin tables' (P3, revisit after PM), 'Plugin test harness — standard pattern for plugin unit + integration tests' (P2, builds alongside PM), and cross-Tower voting is already covered by 'Validate polls federation across two-Tower staging' (P2).

phases.title: Project management plugin — complex SDK consumer
roadmapItems: Project management — second plugin (validates complex SDK case)
phases.notes: Shipped. 9 tasks: schema + CRUD, board list + kanban view, work item CRUD + sidebar preview, DnD reordering, comment threads (useChat), task + board embeds, real-time WS sync, AS2 federation (plugin inbox dispatch hook + outbound delivery + inbound handling), polish pass. SDK additions: <E> component, PluginInboxHandler, fastify.deliverToGroupFollowers. Roadmap item added for e2e federation testing on live Towers.

phases.title: Plugin distribution — npm packaging, admin install, manifest freeze
roadmapItems: Plugin distribution — npm packaging, admin install flow, manifest freeze
Plugin SDK — stable re-exports of @babelr/client utilities
Plugin test harness — standard pattern for plugin unit + integration tests
phases.notes: Polls + PM prove the contract works end-to-end. Phase 5 freezes the manifest, ships npm packaging so third-party authors can build/distribute plugins, and batches the SDK re-export + test harness deferred items. Circle back after MVP gaps are closed.

phases.title: Market-MVP gaps — onboarding, PWA, email verification, 2FA
roadmapItems: First-run onboarding flow — guided server creation + language preference
PWA support — installable web app + offline shell as a quick mobile path
Email verification on registration
Two-factor authentication
phases.notes: Done. All four shipped: onboarding wizard (5-step), PWA (vite-plugin-pwa), email verification (nodemailer + SMTP), 2FA (TOTP + recovery codes).

phases.title: UX refinement — migrate overlays to views, build component primitives
roadmapItems: UX: migrate modal overlays to view registry for mobile-ready layout
UX: component primitives palette (TabbedView, ScrollListView, SidePanel)
phases.notes: Done. All 8 tasks shipped: 3 primitives (TabbedView, ScrollListView, SidePanel), 6 view migrations, auto-toolbar, responsive breakpoints, SDK primitive exports, 7 Playwright e2e tests.

phases.title: Complexity refactor — split god files, extract shared patterns
roadmapItems: Complexity refactor — split god files, extract shared patterns
phases.notes: Done. All 6 tasks shipped: serializers extracted, channels.ts split (1771→488), handleCreate dispatch table, useVoice split (1006→537), PM client split (1212→307), requireAuth + replyError helpers. 18 pre-refactor tests added (274 total).

phases.title: Launch gaps — features users expect from a chat app
roadmapItems: Web push notifications — VAPID, service worker push, desktop Notification API
Link preview / URL unfurling
Pinned messages
User bans — permanent server ban
User profile cards — view other users' profiles on click
phases.notes: Done. All 5 P1 tasks shipped: push notifications (VAPID + desktop fallback), link previews (async og:meta unfurl), pinned messages (generic schema + message-embed cards), user bans (permanent + temp + auto-expiry), profile cards (popover with mutual servers).

phases.title: Launch polish — P2 features for credibility
roadmapItems: Syntax highlighting for code blocks in messages
Advanced search filters — from:user, in:channel, before/after:date, has:file
Do Not Disturb mode + quiet hours
Jump to message — click notification/mention/search result to scroll to message in context
Accessibility — ARIA landmarks, keyboard navigation, screen reader support, focus management
phases.notes: The 'feels polished' cluster. Code highlighting is the quickest win (Shiki/highlight.js). Message jump unlocks pinned message clicks, search result navigation, and notification deep links. Advanced search and DND are independent. Accessibility is the biggest effort but critical for enterprise.

activePhase.title: Launch polish — P2 features for credibility
activePhase.goal: Five P2 features that take Babelr from 'works' to 'feels polished'. Each is independently shippable. Code highlighting and message jump are quickest; accessibility is the biggest effort.
activePhase.sequencing: Code highlighting first (quickest win, biggest visual impact on technical users). Message jump second (unlocks pinned message clicks, search results, notification deep links). Advanced search third (builds on existing full-text infra). DND fourth (extends existing notification preferences). Accessibility last (biggest effort, cross-cutting).

tasks.title: Syntax highlighting for code blocks
tasks.resolution: Shiki integration via lazy-loaded highlighter. 23 common languages pre-loaded (JS/TS/Python/Rust/Go/etc). github-dark theme. Hooked into marked's code renderer (sync path via highlightCodeSync for initial render, preloadHighlighter on app load for warm cache). Applied to both chat messages and wiki content. DOMPurify allowlist updated to permit style attributes for Shiki's token colors. PWA precache scoped to exclude Shiki's 300+ grammar chunks (10 entries / 1.2MB instead of 316 / 10.9MB). 274 tests + build pass.
tasks.details: Add Shiki (or highlight.js) to the markdown renderer for fenced code blocks with language tags (```js, ```python, etc.). Lazy-load the highlighter to avoid bloating the initial bundle. Apply to both chat messages and wiki content. The wiki enhancement roadmap item covers additional wiki-specific features (callouts, task lists, ToC) — this task is just the syntax highlighting shared between chat and wiki.

tasks.title: Jump to message
tasks.resolution: Server: GET /channels/:channelId/messages?around=messageId loads messages before and after a target (half the limit each direction). Client: jumpToMessage(messageId) sets highlightMessageId state, which flows through MessageList → MessageItem. MessageItem scrolls into view (smooth, centered) and applies a 2-second blue flash animation via message-highlighted CSS class. Pinned message cards are clickable — clicking jumps to the message and closes the pins sidebar. API function getMessagesAround added. 274 tests + build pass.
tasks.details: Click a pinned message card, search result, mention, or notification to navigate to the message in context. Server: GET /channels/:channelId/messages?around=messageId returns messages before and after the target. Client: scrollToMessage(channelId, messageId) function that selects the channel, loads the message context, scrolls to it, and highlights it briefly (yellow flash or border pulse). Wire into: pinned message cards (click jumps), mentions view (click jumps), search results (click jumps), push notification deep links (data.channelId + data.messageId).

tasks.title: Advanced search filters
tasks.resolution: Server: parseFilters() extracts from:, in:, before:, after:, has: operators from the query string, adds corresponding SQL conditions (author lookup, channel name ILIKE, date range, attachment/link/image checks). Remaining text goes through existing tsquery full-text. Returns active filters in the response so the client can render pills. Client: SearchPanel shows filter pills with × to remove each filter. Hint text below the search bar shows available operators. All strings through i18n (5 new keys). 274 tests + build pass.
tasks.details: Parse search queries for operator prefixes: from:username, in:channelname, before:YYYY-MM-DD, after:YYYY-MM-DD, has:file, has:image, has:link. Add WHERE clauses to the existing PostgreSQL full-text search query. Client: filter pills below the search bar showing active filters with × to remove. Autocomplete for from: and in: operators using member/channel lists.

tasks.title: Do Not Disturb + quiet hours
tasks.details: Add 'dnd' to the presence status enum (online/away/dnd/offline). When DND: suppress all push notifications and desktop notifications, show a red DND badge on the user's avatar across the app. Quiet hours: auto-enable DND between user-configured times (stored on actor profile). Server: broadcastPushToChannel and showDesktopNotification check DND status before sending. Client: DND toggle in the channel header user menu or settings. Quiet hours config in the Account settings tab.

tasks.title: Accessibility pass
tasks.details: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance pass. ARIA landmarks on major layout regions (nav, main, aside, complementary). Keyboard navigation: Tab through interactive elements, Enter/Space to activate, arrow keys in lists/tabs. Focus management: trap focus in modals, restore focus on close, auto-focus on view transitions. Skip-to-content link. High-contrast mode support via prefers-contrast media query. Reduced-motion via prefers-reduced-motion. Screen reader announcements for live content (aria-live on message list, notification toasts). Test with VoiceOver (macOS) + NVDA (Windows).
